dbbot v0.8.0-beta.1 promotes MySQL 9.7.0 to the default MySQL example version and adds core deployment support for the 9.7 LTS line.

This beta updates the default MySQL package resolution so MySQL Server, MySQL Shell, and MySQL Router all follow the currently published 9.7.0 glibc2.28 Linux artifacts instead of the older 8.4 defaults.

It also adds a dedicated MySQL 9.7 my.cnf template derived from the existing 8.4 path. The 9.7 template removes replica_parallel_type and group_replication_allow_local_lower_version_join, intentionally leaves innodb_log_writer_threads unset so upstream automation can choose the best value, and pins binlog_transaction_dependency_history_size=1000000 so the new upstream default is explicit to operators.

This beta further extends the core deployment path so secure bootstrap, initialize-insecure handling, asynchronous replication, MGR, and InnoDB Cluster version validation all accept MySQL 9.7 where the 8.4 path was already supported.

This beta is scoped to the core MySQL deployment flow. The explicitly named 8.4 clone backup and PITR playbooks remain version-specific and are not yet generalized for MySQL 9.7.
